parts: The last enzyme in the caffeine biosynthesis pathway, which is disappointingly expressed only in plants, converts theobromine to caffeine using this ubiquitous methylation cofactor. In humans, it is also required to convert norepinephrine to epinephrine.

The starting material, xanthosine, is a purine native to humans, but rather than metabolizing it to stimulants, we oxidize it to this worthless compound, which accumulates in gout. Birds excrete nitrogen in the form of this compound.

Humans would probably make more caffeine than plants since we express the enzyme HGPRT, an easy route to xanthine from degraded nucleotides through one of these purine biosynthesis pathways. They are contrasted with de novo routes.

Dufay was an early and prominent practitioner of this homophonic technique, in which three voices move in parallel motion, with the lower two voices a fourth and sixth respectively below the upper voice.
